title: WOS API Auth
description: Public API resource schema for token + session lifecycle (ADR 0082 D-15 step 6). Covers login, logout, refresh, scope-swap, introspection, and current-scope read. Authorization-as-mechanism (the JWT bearer + scope headers per ADR 0082 D-9) is settled; this domain authors the lifecycle endpoints around it. The scope shape (`ScopeContext`) gates on ADR 0068 D-2 (Proposed) — Tenant -> Organization -> Workspace -> Environment per VISION §V; this schema authors against the proposed shape and tolerates server-side absence of fields the deployment has not yet adopted, per ADR 0082 D-15 greenfield discipline. Vendor extensions on closed-with-vendor-extension enums use the `^x-[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$` prefix per ADR 0082 D-12.

ActorRef.description: Stable actor reference from the identity/governance subsystem (ADR 0082 D-9). Hoisted to `_common.schema.json` per PLN-0404.

ActorScope.description: Closed scope-membership tuple (Tenant -> Organization -> Workspace -> Environment) per VISION §V and ADR 0068 D-2 (Proposed). Reused from `actor.schema.json#/$defs/ActorScope` so the wire shape stays canonical across actor identity, scope-context reads, and scope-swap requests (ADR 0082 D-14 — never redefine).

LoginKind.description: Closed-with-vendor-extension login credential family (ADR 0082 D-12). Reserved literals: `password` (username + password); `webauthn` (FIDO2 / passkey assertion — required for respondent applicant identity per VISION §V respondent-side WebAuthn PRF binding for per-class encryption); `oidc-sso` (OpenID Connect single-sign-on hand-off, typical for staff via an external IdP). Vendor extensions MUST use an `^x-[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$` prefix.

MfaChallengeKind.description: Closed-with-vendor-extension second-factor challenge family (ADR 0082 D-12). Reserved literals: `totp` (RFC 6238 time-based OTP); `webauthn` (FIDO2 / passkey assertion as a second factor when the primary credential is `password`); `sms-otp` (SMS one-time code); `email-otp` (email one-time code); `push` (out-of-band push approval). Vendor extensions MUST use an `^x-[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$` prefix. Note: `sms-otp` is recorded as the lowest-assurance literal the vocabulary exposes; high-assurance deployments SHOULD prefer `webauthn` or `totp`.

TokenKind.description: Closed token-kind taxonomy. Closed-no-extension by design — the public WOS API contract carries opaque JWT bearer tokens only (ADR 0082 D-9). The `bearer` literal is a single-element enum to keep the wire shape extensible by major schema bump if a second token kind ever lands.

AuthAssuranceLevel.description: Closed-with-vendor-extension authentication-assurance level (ADR 0082 D-12), aligned with the IdentityAttestation `assuranceLevel` taxonomy from ADR 0068 D-3.1 (Proposed). Reserved literals: `low` (single-factor, low-assurance); `standard` (single-factor with proven possession, e.g. password + verified email); `high` (multi-factor with at least one phishing-resistant factor, e.g. WebAuthn); `very-high` (hardware-bound, identity-proofed). Vendor extensions MUST use an `^x-[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$` prefix.

ScopeContext.description: Current scope context for the bearer token, returned by `GET /api/v1/auth/scope`. Carries the scope tuple per VISION §V and ADR 0068 D-2 (Proposed): Tenant -> Organization -> Workspace -> Environment. The shape reuses `ActorScope` from `actor.schema.json` so the wire form is canonical; per ADR 0082 D-14 inline redefinition would be a contract bug. The `actorRef` field identifies the principal the token authenticates; consumers that need the principal class can read it from the URN's principal-class segment without a second round-trip.

actorRef.description: URN of the principal authenticated by the bearer token.

scope.description: Scope-membership tuple — Tenant / Organization / Workspace? / Environment per VISION §V and ADR 0068 D-2 (Proposed). When ADR 0068 promotes to Accepted this is the wire form scope is reported in; until then, server implementations MAY return only the fields they have settled.

assuranceLevel.description: Authentication-assurance level the bearer token attests.

loginKind.description: Credential family used to obtain the original session. Useful for downstream policy that gates rights-impacting actions on `webauthn`-authenticated sessions.

mfaSatisfied.description: True when the bearer token's session has satisfied a second-factor challenge. Single-factor sessions (legacy / sandbox deployments) MAY return false.

MfaChallenge.description: Pending second-factor challenge returned in `LoginResponse` when the server requires a follow-up MFA step. The `challengeId` is opaque; the client redeems it via a follow-up `POST /api/v1/auth/login` with the same `Idempotency-Key` and the supplied factor's response; the contract for those follow-up shapes lives in a future amendment when the MFA back-and-forth surface ratifies. Today this resource serves as the discoverable seam declaring that an MFA step is REQUIRED.

challengeId.description: Opaque server-issued token identifying the pending MFA challenge. Single-use; expires per `expiresAt`.

kind.description: Closed-with-vendor-extension second-factor family the client must satisfy.

expiresAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp at which the challenge expires (ADR 0069). Clients receiving an expired challenge MUST restart `POST /api/v1/auth/login`.

hint.description: Optional hint displayed to the actor (for example a redacted email or phone number for `email-otp` / `sms-otp` factors). Open prose — UX concern.

LoginRequest.description: Request body for `POST /api/v1/auth/login`. The `Idempotency-Key` HTTP header is REQUIRED per ADR 0082 D-16: login is externally side-effecting (it issues a token, opens a session, may emit an authentication-record event) and a network retry without idempotency would create duplicate sessions. The `kind` field is the discriminator for the credential payload's interpretation; per ADR 0082 D-12 it is closed-with-vendor-extension. Per ADR 0082 D-11 the credential fields are optional on the wire (presence depends on `kind`) but the server validates the kind/payload combination at receipt and rejects mismatches with `WOS-1422`. Note: the contract intentionally carries the credential payload as named-seam fields (`password`, `webauthnAssertion`, `oidcCode`) rather than a discriminated union body so the schema reads as a single closed object — adding a kind requires extending this object plus the `LoginKind` enum.

kind.description: Closed-with-vendor-extension credential family. The accompanying credential field MUST match: `password` -> `username` + `password`; `webauthn` -> `webauthnAssertion`; `oidc-sso` -> `oidcCode`.

username.description: Username for `kind == password` logins. Open string — opaque to the contract; the back-end resolves it to a principal. REQUIRED when `kind == password`.

password.description: Password for `kind == password` logins. Open string — never logged, never echoed back; the server hashes and discards. REQUIRED when `kind == password`.

webauthnAssertion.description: Base64URL-encoded WebAuthn / FIDO2 authenticator assertion (`AuthenticatorAssertionResponse`). REQUIRED when `kind == webauthn`. Open string with named seam — the server validates per the registered authenticator's public key.

oidcCode.description: OpenID Connect authorization code (RFC 6749 §1.3.1) for `kind == oidc-sso` logins. REQUIRED when `kind == oidc-sso`. The server exchanges the code with the configured IdP at receipt.

mfaChallengeId.description: Opaque token from a prior `LoginResponse.mfaChallenge` when this request continues an MFA flow. Omitted on the first request of a flow; required on the redemption request.

mfaResponse.description: Factor-specific response payload (TOTP code, SMS/email OTP, WebAuthn assertion, push approval token). Format depends on `MfaChallenge.kind`. Required when `mfaChallengeId` is present.

requestedScope.description: Optional scope the client is requesting at login (Tenant / Organization / Workspace? / Environment per ADR 0068 D-2 — Proposed). When omitted the server resolves a default scope from the principal's primary membership.

LoginResponse.description: Response body for `POST /api/v1/auth/login`. Carries the issued bearer token (when authentication succeeded), the active scope, expiry, and an optional `mfaChallenge` that the client must redeem before the response carries a token. When `mfaChallenge` is present, `accessToken` is omitted and the client redeems the challenge via a follow-up `POST /api/v1/auth/login`. When `mfaChallenge` is absent, the token is immediately usable.

tokenKind.description: Closed token-kind taxonomy. Always `bearer` at v1.

accessToken.description: Opaque JWT bearer token used as the `Authorization: Bearer <jwt>` header value (ADR 0082 D-9). Omitted when `mfaChallenge` is present — the client MUST satisfy the challenge before receiving a token.

refreshToken.description: Opaque refresh token redeemable at `POST /api/v1/auth/refresh`. Omitted when the deployment does not issue refresh tokens (sandbox profiles) or when `mfaChallenge` is present.

expiresAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 expiry timestamp for `accessToken`. Present iff `accessToken` is present.

scope.description: Active scope tuple at the time of token issuance (ADR 0068 D-2 — Proposed). Echoes `LoginRequest.requestedScope` when supplied; otherwise the server's resolved default.

actorRef.description: URN of the principal the token authenticates. Present iff `accessToken` is present.

assuranceLevel.description: Authentication-assurance level attested by this session, derived from `LoginKind` and any satisfied MFA factor (ADR 0068 D-3.1 — Proposed).

mfaChallenge.description: Pending second-factor challenge. Present iff the deployment requires MFA and the first-factor credential succeeded but a second factor remains.

LogoutRequest.description: Request body for `POST /api/v1/auth/logout`. The `Idempotency-Key` HTTP header is OPTIONAL but RECOMMENDED — repeated logout calls are naturally idempotent on the visible state (the session is gone). Per ADR 0082 D-12 the `scope` enum is closed-no-extension because the choice is normative: vendor extensions would invite local-policy logout semantics that confuse audit trails.

scope.description: Closed logout scope. `this-session`: revoke only the bearer token presented on this request. `all-sessions`: revoke every active session for the bearer principal (the global-logout pattern that bumps `auth_epoch` per the WOS server auth contract — see `workspec-server/crates/wos-server/PARITY.md` ▎ Auth contract). Closed-no-extension by design: vendor-specific logout scopes would create audit ambiguity.

RefreshRequest.description: Request body for `POST /api/v1/auth/refresh`. The `Idempotency-Key` HTTP header is OPTIONAL — refresh is naturally idempotent on `(refreshToken)` for the deployment retention window: a repeat request returns the same fresh `accessToken`/`refreshToken` pair. The contract intentionally keeps the refresh token in the body (not a header or a cookie) so it survives mTLS-style transports without depending on browser context.

refreshToken.description: Opaque refresh token previously issued by `LoginResponse.refreshToken` or a prior `RefreshResponse.refreshToken`. Single-use within the deployment retention window; the server SHOULD rotate the refresh token on each redemption.

RefreshResponse.description: Response body for `POST /api/v1/auth/refresh`. Issues a fresh access token and (typically) a rotated refresh token. The active scope is echoed so clients can confirm the session continues at the expected scope.

tokenKind.description: Closed token-kind taxonomy. Always `bearer` at v1.

accessToken.description: Fresh JWT bearer token replacing the prior `accessToken`.

refreshToken.description: Rotated refresh token replacing the one redeemed by this request. Omitted when the deployment configuration uses non-rotating refresh tokens.

expiresAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 expiry timestamp for the new `accessToken`.

scope.description: Active scope tuple for the refreshed session (ADR 0068 D-2 — Proposed). MUST equal the redeemed token's prior scope; refresh does NOT swap scope.

ScopeSwapRequest.description: Request body for `POST /api/v1/auth/scope-swap`. The `Idempotency-Key` HTTP header is REQUIRED per ADR 0082 D-16: scope-swap is externally side-effecting (it emits a governance audit event and issues a fresh token bound to the new scope). Per ADR 0068 D-2 (Proposed) the case scope bundle `(Tenant, DefinitionId, KernelId, LedgerId)` is immutable for the case lifetime; this endpoint swaps the *session's* scope for actors who hold authority across multiple Organization / Workspace / Environment scopes within a tenant. Cross-tenant swaps are rejected per ADR 0068 D-1 (cross-tenant reads are impossible by construction).

targetScope.description: Target scope tuple the session swaps to (Tenant -> Organization -> Workspace? -> Environment per ADR 0068 D-2 — Proposed). The server validates per-tenant authorization (ADR 0068 D-3 — authority is per-tenant; cross-tenant swaps are rejected with `WOS-1403`). The `tenant` field MUST equal the bearer token's current tenant; mismatches are rejected with `WOS-1403`.

TokenIntrospection.description: Token introspection response returned by `GET /api/v1/auth/introspect` for the bearer token presented on the request. Modeled after RFC 7662 (OAuth 2.0 Token Introspection) with WOS-specific scope and assurance fields. The `active` flag is the load-bearing field: when false, all other fields except possibly `expiresAt` MAY be omitted (the server is asserting the token does not authenticate). Service-account and workload calls (per VISION §V) use this endpoint to verify their token's continuing validity without round-tripping a real read.

active.description: True when the bearer token is currently valid (signature verified, not expired, not revoked, not bumped by a global-logout `auth_epoch` rotation). False otherwise.

tokenKind.description: Closed token-kind taxonomy. Always `bearer` at v1. Omitted when `active == false`.

actorRef.description: URN of the principal the token authenticates. Omitted when `active == false`.

scope.description: Active scope tuple bound to the token (ADR 0068 D-2 — Proposed). Omitted when `active == false`.

issuedAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 timestamp when the token was issued.

expiresAt.description: UTC RFC 3339 expiry timestamp. Present when `active == true`; MAY be present when `active == false` if the deployment chooses to disclose that the token had expired (rather than been revoked).

assuranceLevel.description: Authentication-assurance level attested by this session (ADR 0068 D-3.1 — Proposed). Omitted when `active == false`.

loginKind.description: Credential family used to obtain the original session. Omitted when `active == false`.

mfaSatisfied.description: True when the session has satisfied a second-factor challenge. Omitted when `active == false`.
